title: Piquant Saudi Barley Pie
description: A spicy, juicy barley pie folded into ready rolled shortcrust pastry, served with a fragrant tomato‑barley emulsion, dairy‑free and perfect for a monsoon lunch.
instructions: In a saucepan, heat olive oil over medium heat and sauté onion and garlic until translucent.
Add barley to the pan and stir for 2 minutes, letting it toast lightly.
Pour in vegetable broth and tomato puree, then add cumin, coriander, cardamom, and salt; bring to a simmer.
Cover and cook the barley mixture for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until barley is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Remove from heat, stir in lemon juice, and allow the porridge to cool slightly.
Roll out the shortcrust pastry to fit a 9-inch pie dish.
Place the pastry in the dish, pressing gently to seal the edges.
Divide the barley mixture into the pastry-lined dish, smoothing the top.
Bake the pie for 20 minutes, or until the pastry edges are golden brown.
Let the pie rest for 5 minutes, then garnish with fresh parsley before slicing.
